items.verse: وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَـٰكُمْ ثُمَّ صَوَّرْنَـٰكُمْ ثُمَّ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ ٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِـَٔادَمَ فَسَجَدُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ إِبْلِيسَ لَمْ يَكُن مِّنَ ٱلسَّـٰجِدِينَ
items.verseEn: And We have certainly created you, [O mankind], and given you [human] form. Then We said to the angels, "Prostrate to Adam"; so they prostrated, except for Iblees. He was not of those who prostrated.

items.verse: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ ٱرْكَعُوا۟ لَا يَرْكَعُونَ
items.verseEn: And when it is said to them, "Bow [in prayer]," they do not bow.

items.verse: إِنَّمَا ٱلتَّوْبَةُ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ لِلَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ ٱلسُّوٓءَ بِجَهَـٰلَةٍۢ ثُمَّ يَتُوبُونَ مِن قَرِيبٍۢ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يَتُوبُ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًۭا
items.verseEn: The repentance accepted by Allāh is only for those who do wrong in ignorance [or carelessness] and then repent soon [after]. It is those to whom Allāh will turn in forgiveness, and Allāh is ever Knowing and Wise.

items.verse: وَٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا فَعَلُوا۟ فَـٰحِشَةً أَوْ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ ذَكَرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ فَٱسْتَغْفَرُوا۟ لِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَمَن يَغْفِرُ ٱلذُّنُوبَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ وَلَمْ يُصِرُّوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَا فَعَلُوا۟ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ
items.verseEn: And those who, when they commit an immorality or wrong themselves [by transgression], remember Allāh and seek forgiveness for their sins - and who can forgive sins except Allāh? - and [who] do not persist in what they have done while they know.
So leave them in their confusion for a time.

items.verse: وَلَا تَمْشِ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مَرَحًا ۖ إِنَّكَ لَن تَخْرِقَ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَلَن تَبْلُغَ ٱلْجِبَالَ طُولًۭا
items.verseEn: And do not walk upon the earth exultantly. Indeed, you will never tear the earth [apart], and you will never reach the mountains in height.

items.verse: أَءُلْقِىَ ٱلذِّكْرُ عَلَيْهِ مِنۢ بَيْنِنَا بَلْ هُوَ كَذَّابٌ أَشِرٌۭ
items.verseEn: Has the message been sent down upon him from among us? Rather, he is an insolent liar."

items.verse: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعَالَوْا۟ إِلَىٰ مَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ وَإِلَى ٱلرَّسُولِ رَأَيْتَ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ يَصُدُّونَ عَنكَ صُدُودًۭا
items.verseEn: And when it is said to them, "Come to what Allāh has revealed and to the Messenger," you see the hypocrites turning away from you in aversion.
But when there comes the Deafening Blast

items.verse: كَلَّآ إِذَا دُكَّتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ دَكًّۭا دَكًّۭا
items.verseEn: No! When the earth has been leveled - pounded and crushed

categories.name: مَظَاهِرُ الْقُدْرَةِ الْإِلَٰهِيَّةِ فِي الْمَادِّيَّاتِ
categories.nameEn: Manifestations of Divine Power in Material Forms

items.verse: وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِ ٱلْجَوَارِ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ كَٱلْأَعْلَـٰمِ
items.verseEn: And of His signs are the ships in the sea, like mountains.

items.verse: فَٱرْتَقِبْ يَوْمَ تَأْتِى ٱلسَّمَآءُ بِدُخَانٍۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
items.verseEn: Then watch for the Day when the sky will bring a visible smoke

items.verse: إِنَّا زَيَّنَّا ٱلسَّمَآءَ ٱلدُّنْيَا بِزِينَةٍ ٱلْكَوَاكِبِ
items.verseEn: Indeed, We have adorned the nearest heaven with an adornment of stars
By the star when it descends,

harf.hint: A majestic seat of authority, often associated with divine power.
harf.verse: 20:5 - The Most Merciful [who is] above the Throne established.

deduction.title: The Appointed Leader of a Nation
deduction.intro: In a time of great challenge and despair for a people, a divine appointment was made. This individual, initially overlooked by many, was chosen by Allah to lead a nation against a formidable adversary. Their story is a testament to the power of faith and the wisdom behind divine decree, showing that true leadership is not always found in expected places.
clues: I was chosen by Allah to be a leader for my people when they sought a ruler to fight in His cause.
My people initially questioned my appointment, feeling that others were more deserving due to their wealth or lineage.
A sign of my rightful leadership was the return of a sacred chest, containing relics from previous prophets, which brought tranquility.
I led my army to a river, where I tested their obedience by forbidding them to drink more than a single scoop of water.
Despite the small number of those who remained steadfast after the test, we faced a mighty warrior and their army.
Under my command, a young individual from my army, who was not a renowned fighter, bravely confronted and defeated the formidable enemy champion.

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: The appointment as a leader against a giant
The flood that engulfed the land
trial.answer: The appointment as a leader against a giant

outcome.label: Outcome
options: A kingdom was established with immense wealth and power.
The people were drowned in a great flood.
The enemy's champion was defeated, and Allah granted victory.
A great drought afflicted the land for years.
The leader was swallowed by a large creature.
outcome.answer: The enemy's champion was defeated, and Allah granted victory.

deduction.arabic: فَلَمَّا فَصَلَ طَالُوتُ بِٱلْجُنُودِ قَالَ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُبْتَلِيكُم بِنَهَرٍۢ فَمَن شَرِبَ مِنْهُ فَلَيْسَ مِنِّى وَمَن لَّمْ يَطْعَمْهُ فَإِنَّهُۥ مِنِّىٓ إِلَّا مَنِ ٱغْتَرَفَ غُرْفَةًۢ بِيَدِهِۦ ۚ فَشَرِبُوا۟ مِنْهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلًۭا مِّنْهُمْ ۚ فَلَمَّا جَاوَزَهُۥ هُوَ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ قَالُوا۟ لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا ٱلْيَوْمَ بِجَالُوتَ وَجُنُودِهِۦ ۚ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَظُنُّونَ أَنَّهُم مُّلَـٰقُوا۟ ٱللَّهِ كَم مِّن فِئَةٍۢ قَلِيلَةٍ غَلَبَتْ فِئَةًۭ كَثِيرَةًۢ بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ مَعَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ
deduction.verse: And when Saul went forth with the soldiers, he said, "Indeed, Allāh will be testing you with a river. So whoever drinks from it is not of me, and whoever does not taste it is indeed of me, excepting one who takes [from it] in the hollow of his hand." But they drank from it, except a [very] few of them. Then when he had crossed it along with those who believed with him, they said, "There is no power for us today against Goliath and his soldiers." But those who were certain that they would meet Allāh said, "How many a small company has overcome a large company by permission of Allāh. And Allāh is with the patient." (2:249)

scramble.hint: This verse encourages perseverance and assures believers of divine recompense for their righteous deeds.
scramble.arabic: وَٱصْبِرْ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ
scramble.verseEn: And be patient, for indeed, Allāh does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good.
